differentials.name: Monitor effectiveness of clinical treatment noninvasively
differentials.raw: Monitor effectiveness of clinical treatment noninvasively

differentials.name: symptomatic in 80-90% if iron deposits > 10 g
differentials.raw: symptomatic in 80-90% if iron deposits > 10 g

differentials.name: low serum testosterone → decreased libido, impotence
differentials.raw: low serum testosterone → decreased libido, impotence

differentials.raw: fatigue, amenorrhea, loss of body hair, testicular atrophy

differentials.name: liver iron index > 2
differentials.raw: liver iron index > 2 (= liver iron concentration [µmol per gram of dry weight] per patient's age)

differentials.name: serum Fe > 300 mg/dL; serum transferrin saturation > 50%
differentials.raw: serum Fe > 300 mg/dL; serum transferrin saturation > 50%

differentials.name: Insulin-dependent diabetes mellitus (30-60%) ← insulin resistance by hepatocytes + damage of pancreatic β-cell ← iron deposition
differentials.raw: Insulin-dependent diabetes mellitus (30-60%) ← insulin resistance by hepatocytes + damage of pancreatic β-cell ← iron deposition

differentials.name: Sideroblastic anemia ← impaired protoporphyrin production ◊  NOT in sickle cell anemia
differentials.raw: Sideroblastic anemia ← impaired protoporphyrin production ◊  NOT in sickle cell anemia

differentials.name: Iron overload siderosis ›  secondary to ↑ in iron absorption
differentials.raw: Iron overload siderosis ›  secondary to ↑ in iron absorption

differentials.name: Bantu siderosis = excessive dietary iron from food preparation in iron containers
differentials.raw: Bantu siderosis = excessive dietary iron from food preparation in iron containers (Kaffir beer)

differentials.name: abnormal iron deposition in RES is clinically of little significance
differentials.raw: abnormal iron deposition in RES is clinically of little significance (NO damage of affected organs)

context: =  pathologic state of intracellular iron accumulation / excess iron deposition in various parenchymal organs (liver, pancreas, spleen, kidneys, heart) leading to cirrhosis with portal hypertension Role of imaging: NECT (63% sensitive + 96% specific in iron overload): √  diffuse / rarely focal increase in liver density (> 72 HU) √  depiction of portal + hepatic veins against background of hyperattenuating liver √  dual energy CT (at 80 + 120 kVp) can quantitate amount of iron deposition ◊  Superimposed steatosis reduces sensitivity! ◊  Wilson disease, colloidal gold treatment, long-term amioda
